# The Dummy Genome Project

The Dummy Genome Project was an international research effort. A working draft
was announced in 2003 after sequencing roughly 3,200 megabases of DNA.

Key statistics reported by the project:

- The reference assembly covered approximately 20,500 protein-coding genes.
- Base-calling accuracy reached 99.9% across the finished sequence.
- The total program cost was about $2.7 billion over its lifetime.
- The haploid genome spans 3.2 Gb of sequence.

These figures are used throughout downstream analysis and are considered the
canonical numbers for this dummy dataset.
